title: Triticum durum grex leucura
intro: Triticum durum grex leucura, commonly known as Durum wheat or Leucurum, is an ancient variety of wheat native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its high protein content and distinctive white or light-colored grains. The health benefits of Durum wheat include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve blood sugar control, and provide a rich source of essential amino acids and fiber, making it a nutritious addition to a balanced diet. The bioactive constituents of Durum wheat include various phenolic compounds, such as ferulic acid and avenanthramides, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Traditionally, Durum wheat is used to make various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, often combined with other herbs to create remedies for digestive issues and to support overall cardiovascular health.
taxonomy: Triticum durum grex leucura is a subspecies of durum wheat, which belongs to the family Poaceae. It is a member of the genus Triticum and is classified as a subspecies of the species Triticum durum. The species Triticum durum is further classified as a member of the genus Triticum, which includes various species of wheat. Within the family Poaceae, Triticum durum grex leucura is part of the order Poales. At the kingdom level, it is classified as Plantae.
constituents: Triticum durum grex leucura, also known as durum wheat, is a type of ancient grain that contains various active constituents. These constituents include proteins, such as gliadin and glutenin, which are responsible for its nutritional and functional properties. It also contains fiber, vitamins, and minerals like iron, zinc, and selenium, making it a nutritious food source. Additionally, durum wheat contains phenolic compounds, including ferulic acid and gallic acid, which have been shown to have antioxidant properties. The exact composition of active constituents in Triticum durum grex leucura may vary depending on factors like soil quality and climate.
